metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4896
metadata.comment: Rationale: "Sarm1 knockdown also influenced the number and morphology of dendritic spines in mature neurons. In Sarm1 knockdown neurons, mushroom-shaped or stubby dendritic spines were still present, but with lower density along dendrites (Fig. 5A and B). We noticed that the remaining dendritic spines in Sarm1 knockdown neurons are longer and that their spine heads are larger compared with those in neurons transfected with vector control (Fig. 5, A and B). These data suggest that Sarm1 also influences the density and morphology of dendritic spines.". Experimental description: "Hippocampal neurons from E18-19 rat embryos were cultured and transfected via the calcium phosphate method..." (p. 782)
